```{r}
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_1_40_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.2912, 0.7088)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5806

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 83.33%

Specificity = 16.67%

### C5.0B Model 2

Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_2_40_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_2_40_emotion)
```

# Additional Models

It is a good idea to train a few more classifiers (non-decision tree) to see how they compare to the current decision tree models. This allows us to see whether there is a significant difference between decision tree and non-decision tree classifiers.

## SVM

Support vector machines are a good choice as they are also non-linear but operate in a different way to decision trees (geometrically finds the optimal hyperplane that maximises the margin between different classes).

```{r}
# No CV setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

svm_model1 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on 20% test set:

```{r}
svm_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_performance)
```

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   29   18
      fake   18   29
                                         
               Accuracy : 0.617          
                 95% CI : (0.511, 0.7154)
    No Information Rate : 0.5            
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.01488        
                                         
                  Kappa : 0.234          
                                         
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1.00000        
                                         
            Sensitivity : 0.6170         
            Specificity : 0.6170         
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6170         
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6170         
             Prevalence : 0.5000         
         Detection Rate : 0.3085         
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5000         
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6170  
```

## Linear Regression

This will be the linear benchmark to compare our other non-linear model performances to. A comparable performance might suggest that the data is not non-linear and is best captured by linear models.

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

lr_model1 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test set:

```{r}
lr_model1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_performance)
```

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   24   18
      fake   23   29
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5638          
                 95% CI : (0.4576, 0.6659)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1282          
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1277          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.5322          
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.5106          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5577          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5714          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5532          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5638  
```

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
lr_model1_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_40_performance)
```

## SVM (CV)

```{r}
# Cross-valiation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

svm_model1_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)


# No native feature importance function.
```

Evaluate on 20% test set:

```{r}
svm_model_1_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(svm_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

5 folds, 3 repeats:

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   31   18
      fake   16   29
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6383          
                 95% CI : (0.5327, 0.7349)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.004774        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.2766          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.863832        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.6596          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6444          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6327          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.4787          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6383  
```

10 folds, 3 repeats:

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   30   18
      fake   17   29
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6277          
                 95% CI : (0.5218, 0.7252)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.008605        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.2553          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1.000000        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.6383          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6304          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6250          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.4894          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6277 
```

## Logistic Regression (CV)

```{r}
# Cross-valiation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

lr_model1_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)

plot(caret::varImp(lr_model1_cv)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on 20% test set:

```{r}
lr_model1_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(lr_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

5 folds, 3 repeats:

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   24   18
      fake   23   29
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5638          
                 95% CI : (0.4576, 0.6659)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1282          
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1277          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.5322          
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.5106          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5577          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5714          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5532          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5638          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake    
```

10 folds, 3 repeats:

```         
          Reference
Prediction real fake
      real   24   18
      fake   23   29
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5638          
                 95% CI : (0.4576, 0.6659)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1282          
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1277          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.5322          
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.5106          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5577          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5714          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5532          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5638 
```

## LDA (CV)

Linear Discriminant Analysis is another linear model to compare against Logistic Regression. It assumes features are normally distributed and have equal variance.

```{r}
# Cross-valiation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final"
)

set.seed(12345)

lda_model_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "lda",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on 20% test set:

```{r}
lda_model_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lda_model_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type="raw"),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lda_model_cv_performance)
```

```         


          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   29   22
      real   18   25
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5745          
                 95% CI : (0.4682, 0.6759)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.08983         
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1489          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.63526         
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6170          
            Specificity : 0.5319          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5686          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5814          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3085          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5426          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5745          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake    
```

# Figures

Human vs model performance bar chart:

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=6}
ggplot(
  data = data.frame(
    Stimuli = factor(
      c("Full Stimuli", "Point Light Display", "Point Light Display"),
      levels = c("Full Stimuli", "Point Light Display")
    ),
    Method = factor(
      c("Human Observers", "Human Observers", "Random Forest"),
      levels = c("Human Observers", "Random Forest")
    ),
    Accuracy = c(88.33, 50.51, 55.00),
    CI_low  = c(80.0,  40.3,  39.8),
    CI_high = c(93.5,  60.7,  69.3)
  ),
  aes(x = Stimuli, y = Accuracy, fill = Method)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = CI_low, ymax = CI_high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.2, size = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "red", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 50, label = "NIR (0.5)",
           hjust = -1.11, vjust = -0.6, size = 4, colour = "red") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Human Observers" = "gray40",
                               "Random Forest"   = "gray70")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Accuracy (%)", fill = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )
```

ROC curve figure:

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Probabilities on the same test set
p_rf  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv,  newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
p_c50 <- predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
p_svm <- predict(svm_model1_cv,     newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
y_test <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))

roc_df <- function(truth, prob, name){
  r <- pROC::roc(truth, prob, levels = c("real","fake"), direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)
  c <- pROC::coords(r, x = "all", ret = c("specificity","sensitivity","threshold"), transpose = FALSE)
  c$model <- name
  c$FPR <- 1 - c$specificity
  c$AUC <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(r))
  c
}

roc_all <- bind_rows(
  roc_df(y_test, p_rf,  "Random Forest"),
  roc_df(y_test, p_c50, "C5.0"),
  roc_df(y_test, p_svm, "SVM (RBF)")
)

# Legend labels with AUC
auc_labels <- roc_all |>
  group_by(model) |>
  summarise(AUC = unique(round(AUC, 3)), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(label = paste0(model, " (AUC=", sprintf("%.3f", AUC), ")"))
label_map <- setNames(auc_labels$label, auc_labels$model)

ggplot(roc_all, aes(x = FPR, y = sensitivity, color = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linewidth = 0.6, colour = "grey60") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0,1), ylim = c(0,1), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                     labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                     labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_color_brewer(type = "qual", palette = "Dark2",
                     labels = label_map, breaks = names(label_map)) +
  labs(
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
    color = NULL,
    title = "ROC Curves on 20% Test Set",
    subtitle = "Random Forest, C5.0, and SVM (positive class = fake)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
library(ROCR)

# Extract predicted probabilities (fake class)
rf_probs  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv,  newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
c50_probs <- predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
svm_probs <- predict(svm_model1_cv,     newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
lr_probs <- predict(lr_model1_cv,     newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

# True outcome (must be numeric 0/1)
true_class <- ifelse(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal == "fake", 1, 0)

# Build ROCR prediction objects
pred_rf  <- prediction(rf_probs,  true_class)
pred_c50 <- prediction(c50_probs, true_class)
pred_svm <- prediction(svm_probs, true_class)
pred_lr <- prediction(lr_probs, true_class)

# Performance objects for TPR/FPR
perf_rf  <- performance(pred_rf,  "tpr", "fpr")
perf_c50 <- performance(pred_c50, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_svm <- performance(pred_svm, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_lr <- performance(pred_lr, "tpr", "fpr")

# Compute AUCs
auc_rf  <- performance(pred_rf,  "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_c50 <- performance(pred_c50, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_svm <- performance(pred_svm, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_lr <- performance(pred_lr, "auc")@y.values[[1]]


# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig2_panel_B.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1), mgp = c(2, 0.7, 0))

# Plot base ROC curvs
plot(perf_rf,  col = "#E69F00", lwd = 2,
     main = "ROC Curves on Test Set (n=94)",
     xlab = "False Positive Rate", ylab = "True Positive Rate")

plot(perf_c50, col = "#009E73", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_svm, col = "#CC79A7",   lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_lr, col = "#0072B2", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

# Diagonal reference line
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "#999999", lty = 2)

# Legend with AUC values
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste0("Random Forest (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_rf), ")"),
         paste0("C5.0 (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_c50), ")"),
         paste0("SVM (RBF) (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_svm), ")"),
         paste0("LR (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_lr), ")")
       ),
       col = c("#E69F00", "#009E73", "#CC79A7", "#0072B2"),
       lwd = 2, cex = 0.9, bty = "n")

dev.off()
```

Class separability:

```{r, fig.width = 5, fig.height = 5}
rf_probs_df <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(
    truth     = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    p_fake_rf = predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
  ) %>%
  select(truth, p_fake_rf)


# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig2_panel_C.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

# Plot densities
ggplot(rf_probs_df, aes(x = p_fake_rf, fill = truth, linetype = truth)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, adjust = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    name = NULL, labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("real" = "solid", "fake" = "dashed"),
    name = NULL, labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Predicted probability of 'fake' (Random Forest)",
    y = "Density",
    title = stringr::str_wrap("Distribution of Predicted Probabilities by True Class",
                              width = 40)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14)
  )
dev.off()

```

Decision boundary map:

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
# two features to plot
f1 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"
f2 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"

df_plot <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")))

# Dense grid covering the observed feature range
make_seq <- function(x, n = 400) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  # tiny expansion to avoid white edge slivers
  seq(rng[1] - 1e-6, rng[2] + 1e-6, length.out = n)
}

grid_2d <- expand.grid(
  setNames(list(make_seq(df_plot[[f1]]), make_seq(df_plot[[f2]])), c(f1, f2)),
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Build full newdata with all predictors present
proto <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[1, , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full <- proto[rep(1, nrow(grid_2d)), , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full[[f1]] <- grid_2d[[f1]]
newdata_full[[f2]] <- grid_2d[[f2]]

# Predict CLASS for background and PROB for the contour
#    type = "raw" -> class labels; type = "prob" -> probabilities
grid_2d$pred_class <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "raw")
probs <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "prob")
grid_2d$prob_fake <- probs[, "fake"]

grid_2d$pred_class <- factor(grid_2d$pred_class, levels = c("real","fake"))

# Plot categorical decision regions + 0.5 contour + points
ggplot() +
  # categorical regions (background)
  geom_raster(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], fill = pred_class),
    alpha = 0.45
  ) +
  # decision boundary at P(fake)=0.5
  geom_contour(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], z = prob_fake),
    breaks = 0.5,
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.85
  ) +
  # data points: shape and fill both mapped to same variable
  geom_point(
    data = df_plot,
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 2.8,
    colour = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  # unified scales for fill and shape
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c("real" = "#1b9e77", "fake" = "#d95f02"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = f1, y = f2,
    title = "Random Forest Decision Boundaries"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

```

Same as before but including training data points:

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
library(ggnewscale)

# two features to plot
f1 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"
f2 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"

# Data for TEST points
df_plot_test <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")))

# Data for TRAINING points
df_plot_train <- dta_classifier_train_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")))

# Dense grid covering the observed feature range
make_seq <- function(x, var_name, n = 400) {
  data_rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Check the variable name string to set plot-specific ranges
  plot_rng <- if (identical(f1, var_name)) {
    c(-2, 2)
  } else if (identical(f2, var_name)) {
    c(-4, 2)
  } else {
    data_rng
  }
  
  # Use the widest range for the grid
  final_rng <- c(min(data_rng[1], plot_rng[1]), max(data_rng[2], plot_rng[2]))
  
  seq(final_rng[1] - 1e-6, final_rng[2] + 1e-6, length.out = n)
}
grid_2d <- expand.grid(
  setNames(list(make_seq(df_plot_test[[f1]], var_name = f1), 
                make_seq(df_plot_test[[f2]], var_name = f2)), 
           c(f1, f2)),
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
proto <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[1, , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full <- proto[rep(1, nrow(grid_2d)), , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full[[f1]] <- grid_2d[[f1]]
newdata_full[[f2]] <- grid_2d[[f2]]
grid_2d$pred_class <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "raw")
probs <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "prob")
grid_2d$prob_fake <- probs[, "fake"]
grid_2d$pred_class <- factor(grid_2d$pred_class, levels = c("real","fake"))



# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig2_panel_E.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

# Plot
ggplot() +
  # categorical regions (background)
  geom_raster(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], fill = pred_class),
    alpha = 0.45,
    show.legend = FALSE 
  ) +
  # decision boundary at P(fake)=0.5
  geom_contour(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], z = prob_fake),
    breaks = 0.5,
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.85
  ) +

  # TEST DATA POINTS & LEGEND
  geom_point(
    data = df_plot_test,
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 2.8,
    colour = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  # Scales for Test Data
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Test",
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.8, alpha = 1))
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "Test",
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.8, alpha = 1))
  ) +

  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  ggnewscale::new_scale("shape") +

  geom_point(
    data = df_plot_train, 
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 0.4,
    colour = "#000000",
    stroke = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Train",
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4))
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "Train",
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4))
  ) +

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(-2, 2),
    ylim = c(-4, 2),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = f1, y = f2,
    title = "Random Forest Decision Boundaries"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dev.off()
```

Feature importance for RF Model 1:

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
# Extract variable importance
vi <- caret::varImp(rf_fit_model1_cv)
vi_df <- vi$importance %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Feature") %>%
  arrange(desc(Overall))
# Shorten feature names
vi_df <- vi_df %>%
  mutate(Feature = stringr::str_replace(Feature, "(AU\\d+)_.*", "\\1"))


# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig2_panel_D.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

ggplot(vi_df, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Overall), y = Overall)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#009E73") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Overall, 2)), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "Feature Importance - Random Forest",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "black")
  )

dev.off()
```

Classifier confusion matrices:

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=4}
# Get predictions for all 4 models on the full test set
pred_rf  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv,  newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled)
pred_c50 <- predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled)
pred_svm <- predict(svm_model1_cv,     newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled)
pred_lr  <- predict(lr_model1_cv,      newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled)

# Get the true, actual class
actual_truth <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal

# Create the raw confusion matrix tables
cm_rf  <- table(Predicted = pred_rf,  Actual = actual_truth)
cm_c50 <- table(Predicted = pred_c50, Actual = actual_truth)
cm_svm <- table(Predicted = pred_svm, Actual = actual_truth)
cm_lr  <- table(Predicted = pred_lr,  Actual = actual_truth)

# Tidy function
tidy_cm <- function(cm, title) {
  as.data.frame(cm) %>%
    mutate(Model = title) 
}

# Bind all 4 tables into one data frame
df_cm_models <- bind_rows(
  tidy_cm(cm_rf,  "Random Forest"),
  tidy_cm(cm_c50, "C5.0"),
  tidy_cm(cm_svm, "SVM (RBF)"),
  tidy_cm(cm_lr,  "Logistic Regression")
)

# Optional: Set factor order for the plot
df_cm_models$Model <- factor(df_cm_models$Model, 
    levels = c("Random Forest", "C5.0", "SVM (RBF)", "Logistic Regression"))

# Calculate percentages PER MODEL
df_cm_models <- df_cm_models %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  mutate(
    Total = sum(Freq),
    Percent = Freq / Total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()



# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig2_panel_F.pdf", width = 10, height = 4)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

ggplot(df_cm_models, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
  # Use black outlines
  geom_tile(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) + 
  
  # Use sprintf to add percentages
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d\n(%.0f%%)", Freq, 100*Percent)),
            size = 5, lineheight = 0.95, color = "black") + 
            
  scale_fill_gradient(high = "#009E73", low = "white") +
  
  # Add coord_equal for square tiles
  coord_equal() + 
  
  facet_wrap(~Model, nrow = 1) + 
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrices by Classifier",
       subtitle = "Performance on Test Set (n=94)",
       fill = "Count") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  
  # Updated theme to match agreement matrices
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black")
  )
dev.off()
```

ROC Curves by Emotion:

```{r, fig.width = 10, fig.height=5}
library(ROCR) 

emotion_test_set <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "yes")
no_emotion_test_set <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "no")

# Extract predicted probabilities (fake class)
rf_probs_em  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv,  newdata = emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
c50_probs_em <- predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
svm_probs_em <- predict(svm_model1_cv,     newdata = emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
lr_probs_em <- predict(lr_model1_cv, newdata = emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

# True outcome (must be numeric 0/1)
true_class_em <- ifelse(emotion_test_set$isfakeorreal == "fake", 1, 0)

# Build ROCR prediction objects
pred_rf_em  <- prediction(rf_probs_em,  true_class_em)
pred_c50_em <- prediction(c50_probs_em, true_class_em)
pred_svm_em <- prediction(svm_probs_em, true_class_em)
pred_lr_em <- prediction(lr_probs_em, true_class_em)

# Performance objects for TPR/FPR
perf_rf_em  <- performance(pred_rf_em,  "tpr", "fpr")
perf_c50_em <- performance(pred_c50_em, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_svm_em <- performance(pred_svm_em, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_lr_em <- performance(pred_lr_em, "tpr", "fpr")

# Compute AUCs
auc_rf_em  <- performance(pred_rf_em,  "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_c50_em <- performance(pred_c50_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_svm_em <- performance(pred_svm_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_lr_em <- performance(pred_lr_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]

# Extract predicted probabilities (fake class)
rf_probs_no_em  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv,  newdata = no_emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
c50_probs_no_em <- predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = no_emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
svm_probs_no_em <- predict(svm_model1_cv,     newdata = no_emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]
lr_probs_no_em <- predict(lr_model1_cv, newdata = no_emotion_test_set, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

# True outcome (must be numeric 0/1)
true_class_no_em <- ifelse(no_emotion_test_set$isfakeorreal == "fake", 1, 0)

# Build ROCR prediction objects
pred_rf_no_em  <- prediction(rf_probs_no_em,  true_class_no_em)
pred_c50_no_em <- prediction(c50_probs_no_em, true_class_no_em)
pred_svm_no_em <- prediction(svm_probs_no_em, true_class_no_em)
pred_lr_no_em <- prediction(lr_probs_no_em, true_class_no_em)

# Performance objects for TPR/FPR
perf_rf_no_em  <- performance(pred_rf_no_em,  "tpr", "fpr")
perf_c50_no_em <- performance(pred_c50_no_em, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_svm_no_em <- performance(pred_svm_no_em, "tpr", "fpr")
perf_lr_no_em <- performance(pred_lr_no_em, "tpr", "fpr")

# Compute AUCs
auc_rf_no_em  <- performance(pred_rf_no_em,  "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_c50_no_em <- performance(pred_c50_no_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_svm_no_em <- performance(pred_svm_no_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]
auc_lr_no_em <- performance(pred_lr_no_em, "auc")@y.values[[1]]


# Plot
# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig3_panel_C_D.pdf", width = 10, height = 5)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1, mgp = c(1.8, 0.7, 0)) # mar adjusts margins

# Emotion vids first
plot(perf_rf_em,  col = "#E69F00", lwd = 2,
     main = "Emotion Videos (n=58)",
     xlab = "False Positive Rate", ylab = "True Positive Rate")

plot(perf_c50_em, col = "#009E73", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_svm_em, col = "#CC79A7",   lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_lr_em, col = "#0072B2", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

# Diagonal reference line
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "#999999", lty = 2)

# Legend with AUC values
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste0("Random Forest (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_rf_em), ")"),
         paste0("C5.0 (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_c50_em), ")"),
         paste0("SVM (RBF) (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_svm_em), ")"),
         paste0("LR (AUC =", sprintf("%.3f", auc_lr_em), ")")
       ),
       col = c("#E69F00", "#009E73", "#CC79A7", "#0072B2"),
       lwd = 2, cex = 0.8, bty = "n") # cex=0.8 makes text slightly smaller


# No emotion vids
plot(perf_rf_no_em,  col = "#E69F00", lwd = 2,
     main = "No Emotion Videos (n=36)",
     xlab = "False Positive Rate", ylab = "True Positive Rate")

plot(perf_c50_no_em, col = "#009E73", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_svm_no_em, col = "#CC79A7",   lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(perf_lr_no_em, col = "#0072B2", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

# Diagonal reference line
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "#999999", lty = 2)

# Legend with AUC values
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste0("Random Forest (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_rf_no_em), ")"),
         paste0("C5.0 (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_c50_no_em), ")"),
         paste0("SVM (RBF) (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_svm_no_em), ")"),
         paste0("LR (AUC = ", sprintf("%.3f", auc_lr_no_em), ")")
       ),
       col = c("#E69F00", "#009E73", "#CC79A7", "#0072B2"),
       lwd = 2, cex = 0.8, bty = "n")

# Reset plotting area to default (1x1)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
dev.off()
```

Density plots by Emotion:

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
emotion_test_set <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "yes")
no_emotion_test_set <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "no")

# Emotion vids
rf_probs_df_em <- emotion_test_set %>%
  mutate(
    truth     = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    p_fake_rf = predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = ., type = "prob")[,"fake"]
  ) %>%
  select(truth, p_fake_rf)

# Calculate densities
# We use from=0, to=1 to keep the density calculation within the 0-1 probability range
dens_em_real <- density(rf_probs_df_em$p_fake_rf[rf_probs_df_em$truth == "real"], from = 0, to = 1, adjust = 1)
dens_em_fake <- density(rf_probs_df_em$p_fake_rf[rf_probs_df_em$truth == "fake"], from = 0, to = 1, adjust = 1)


# No Emotion vids
rf_probs_df_no_em <- no_emotion_test_set %>%
  mutate(
    truth     = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    p_fake_rf = predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = ., type = "prob")[,"fake"]
  ) %>%
  select(truth, p_fake_rf)

# Calculate densities
dens_no_em_real <- density(rf_probs_df_no_em$p_fake_rf[rf_probs_df_no_em$truth == "real"], from = 0, to = 1, adjust = 1)
dens_no_em_fake <- density(rf_probs_df_no_em$p_fake_rf[rf_probs_df_no_em$truth == "fake"], from = 0, to = 1, adjust = 1)

# Plot
# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig3_panel_E_F.pdf", width = 10, height = 5)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1, mgp = c(1.7, 0.7, 0))

col_real <- "#009E73"
col_fake <- "#E69F00"
# Create transparent versions
col_real_fill <- adjustcolor(col_real, alpha.f = 0.4)
col_fake_fill <- adjustcolor(col_fake, alpha.f = 0.4)


# Get Y-axis limits to fit both densities
#ylim_em <- range(0, dens_em_real$y, dens_em_fake$y)

# Shared y-axis limit for both plots
ylim_shared <- range(0, dens_em_real$y, dens_em_fake$y, 
                        dens_no_em_real$y, dens_no_em_fake$y)

# Set up the empty plot
plot(NULL,
     main = "Emotion Videos (n=58)",
     xlab = "Predicted probability of 'fake'", ylab = "Density",
     xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = ylim_shared
)

# Add shaded polygons
polygon(dens_em_real, col = col_real_fill, border = NA)
polygon(dens_em_fake, col = col_fake_fill, border = NA)

# Add lines on top
lines(dens_em_real, lty = "solid", lwd = 2, col = "black")
lines(dens_em_fake, lty = "dashed", lwd = 2, col = "black")

# Add vline and legend
abline(v = 0.5, lty = "dotted", lwd = 2, col = "black")
legend("topright",
       legend = c("Real", "Fake"),
       fill = c(col_real_fill, col_fake_fill),
       border = c("black", "black"),
       lty = c("solid", "dashed"),
       lwd = 2,
       bty = "n", 
       cex = 0.9
)


# Get Y-axis limits
#ylim_no_em <- range(0, dens_no_em_real$y, dens_no_em_fake$y)

# Set up the empty plot
plot(NULL,
     main = "No Emotion Videos (n=36)",
     xlab = "Predicted probability of 'fake'", ylab = "Density",
     xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = ylim_shared
)

# Add shaded polygons
polygon(dens_no_em_real, col = col_real_fill, border = NA)
polygon(dens_no_em_fake, col = col_fake_fill, border = NA)

# Add lines on top
lines(dens_no_em_real, lty = "solid", lwd = 2, col = "black")
lines(dens_no_em_fake, lty = "dashed", lwd = 2, col = "black")

# Add vline and legend
abline(v = 0.5, lty = "dotted", lwd = 2, col = "black")
legend("topright",
       legend = c("Real", "Fake"),
       fill = c(col_real_fill, col_fake_fill),
       border = c("black", "black"),
       lty = c("solid", "dashed"),
       lwd = 2,
       bty = "n",
       cex = 0.9
)

# Reset plotting area to default (1x1)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
dev.off()
```

Decision boundary map by Emotion:

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
# two features to plot (same as before)
f1 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"
f2 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"

# grid calculation
make_seq <- function(x, n = 400) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  seq(rng[1] - 1e-6, rng[2] + 1e-6, length.out = n)
}

grid_2d <- expand.grid(
  setNames(list(make_seq(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[[f1]]), 
                make_seq(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[[f2]])), c(f1, f2)),
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

proto <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[1, , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full <- proto[rep(1, nrow(grid_2d)), , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full[[f1]] <- grid_2d[[f1]]
newdata_full[[f2]] <- grid_2d[[f2]]

grid_2d$pred_class <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "raw")
probs <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "prob")
grid_2d$prob_fake <- probs[, "fake"]
grid_2d$pred_class <- factor(grid_2d$pred_class, levels = c("real","fake"))


df_plot_faceted <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(
    truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    Condition = factor(emotion, 
                       levels = c("yes", "no"), 
                       labels = c("Emotion Videos (n=58)", "No Emotion Videos (n=36)"))
  )


# Plot
ggplot() +
  # background
  geom_raster(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], fill = pred_class),
    alpha = 0.45
  ) +
  # boundary
  geom_contour(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], z = prob_fake),
    breaks = 0.5,
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.85
  ) +
  # data points
  geom_point(
    data = df_plot_faceted, 
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 2.8,
    colour = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake")
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Condition) +
  
  labs(
    x = f1, y = f2,
    title = "Random Forest Decision Boundaries by Emotional Content"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )
```

Decision boundary map by Emotion with train data overlay:

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
library(ggnewscale)

# two features to plot
f1 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"
f2 <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"

df_plot_test <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(
    truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    Condition = factor(emotion, 
                       levels = c("yes", "no"), 
                       labels = c("Emotion Videos (n=58)", "No Emotion Videos (n=36)")) 
  )

df_plot_train <- dta_classifier_train_new_scaled %>%
  mutate(
    truth = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake")),
    Condition = factor(emotion, 
                       levels = c("yes", "no"), 
                       labels = c("Emotion Videos (n=58)", "No Emotion Videos (n=36)"))
  )
  
make_seq <- function(x, var_name, n = 400) {
  data_rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  plot_rng <- if (identical(f1, var_name)) {
    c(-2, 2)
  } else if (identical(f2, var_name)) {
    c(-4, 2)
  } else {
    data_rng
  }
  final_rng <- c(min(data_rng[1], plot_rng[1]), max(data_rng[2], plot_rng[2]))
  seq(final_rng[1] - 1e-6, final_rng[2] + 1e-6, length.out = n)
}
grid_2d <- expand.grid(
  setNames(list(make_seq(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[[f1]], var_name = f1), 
                make_seq(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[[f2]], var_name = f2)), 
           c(f1, f2)),
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
proto <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[1, , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full <- proto[rep(1, nrow(grid_2d)), , drop = FALSE]
newdata_full[[f1]] <- grid_2d[[f1]]
newdata_full[[f2]] <- grid_2d[[f2]]
grid_2d$pred_class <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "raw")
probs <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = newdata_full, type = "prob")
grid_2d$prob_fake <- probs[, "fake"]
grid_2d$pred_class <- factor(grid_2d$pred_class, levels = c("real","fake"))

# Plot
# Save to PDF for paper
#pdf("fig3_panel_G_H.pdf", width = 8, height = 5)

ggplot() +
  # Background and boundary 
  geom_raster(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], fill = pred_class),
    alpha = 0.45,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_contour(
    data = grid_2d,
    aes(x = .data[[f1]], y = .data[[f2]], z = prob_fake),
    breaks = 0.5, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.85
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = df_plot_test, 
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 2.8,
    colour = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Test", 
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.8, alpha = 1)) 
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "Test", 
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.8, alpha = 1)) 
  ) +

  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  ggnewscale::new_scale("shape") +

  geom_point(
    data = df_plot_train, 
    aes(
      x = .data[[f1]],
      y = .data[[f2]],
      shape = truth,
      fill = truth
    ),
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 0.4, 
    colour = "black",
    stroke = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Train", 
    values = c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00"),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4)) 
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "Train", 
    values = c("real" = 21, "fake" = 24),
    labels = c("Real", "Fake"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4)) 
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ Condition) + 
  
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(-2, 2),
    ylim = c(-4, 2),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = f1, y = f2,
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )
#dev.off()
```

Emotion bar chart revised:

```{r, fig.width=3, fig.height=5}
# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig3_panel_A.pdf", width = 3, height = 5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

ggplot(
  data = data.frame(
    Condition = factor(c("Emotion", "No Emotion"),
                       levels = c("Emotion", "No Emotion")),
    Accuracy  = c(72.4, 55.6),
    CI_low    = c(59.8, 39.6),
    CI_high   = c(82.2, 70.5),
    p_label   = c("p < .001", "p = .309"),
    vjust_val = c(-4, -6)
  ),
  aes(x = Condition, y = Accuracy, fill = Condition) 
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black") + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_low, ymax = CI_high),
                width = 0.2, size = 0.8, color = "black") + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "black", size = 0.6) + 
  annotate("text", x = 2.2, y = 50, label = "",
           hjust = -1.1, vjust = -0.5, size = 4, colour = "black") +
  
  geom_text(aes(label = p_label, vjust = vjust_val), size = 5) + 
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Emotion" = "#009E73", "No Emotion" = "#009E73"),
    guide = "none" 
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(y = "Accuracy (%)", x = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 12, color = "black", margin = margin(t=5)), 
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"), 
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )
dev.off()
```

Updated PLD vs Full Stim bar chart:

```{r, fig.width=6, fig.height=6}
ggplot(
  data = data.frame(
    Stimuli = factor(
      c("Full Stimuli", "Point Light Display", "Point Light Display"),
      levels = c("Full Stimuli", "Point Light Display")
    ),
    Method = factor(
      c("Human Observers", "Random Forest", "Human Observers"),
      levels = c("Human Observers", "Random Forest")
    ),
    Accuracy = c(88.33, 55.00, 50.51),
    CI_low  = c(80.0,  39.8,  40.3),
    CI_high = c(93.5,  69.3,  60.7)
  ),
  aes(x = Stimuli, y = Accuracy, fill = Method)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), width = 0.6, color = "black") + # <-- Added outline
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = CI_low, ymax = CI_high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.2, size = 0.8, color = "black" # <-- Set color to black
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "black", size = 0.6) + # <-- Changed to black
  annotate("text", x = 1.55, y = 50, label = "NIR (0.5)", # <-- Adjusted x-position
           hjust = -2.7, vjust = -0.5, size = 4, colour = "black") + # <-- Changed to black
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  
  # --- Use your established color palette ---
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Human Observers" = "#1b9e77",
                               "Random Forest"   = "#d95f02")) +
  
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Accuracy (%)", fill = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )
```

Updated Emotion vs No Emotion confusion matrices:

```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
# Filter subsets
emotion_test <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "yes")
no_emotion_test <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>% filter(emotion == "no")

# Model predictions
pred_emotion    <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = emotion_test)
pred_no_emotion <- predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = no_emotion_test)

# Raw confusion matrices
cm_emotion <- table(Predicted = pred_emotion, Actual = emotion_test$isfakeorreal)
cm_no_emotion <- table(Predicted = pred_no_emotion, Actual = no_emotion_test$isfakeorreal)

# Convert to tidy format
tidy_cm <- function(cm, title) {
  as.data.frame(cm) %>%
    mutate(Condition = title)
}

df_cm <- bind_rows(
  tidy_cm(cm_emotion,    "Emotion Videos"),
  tidy_cm(cm_no_emotion, "No Emotion Videos")
)

# calculate percentages PER FACET
# This calculates the % within each 2x2 grid
df_cm <- df_cm %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  mutate(
    Total = sum(Freq),
    Percent = Freq / Total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


p_cm <- df_cm %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
  
  # black outlines
  geom_tile(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  
  # percentages
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d\n(%.0f%%)", Freq, 100*Percent)),
            size = 5, lineheight = 0.95, color = "black") +
            
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#009E73") +
  
  # Add coord_equal for square tiles
  coord_equal() +
  
  facet_wrap(~Condition, nrow = 1) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrices by Emotional Content",
       subtitle = "Performance on Test Set (n=94)",
       fill = "Count") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  
  # Updated theme to match agreement matrices
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"), 
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black")
  )

# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig3_panel_B.pdf", width=7, height=5)
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
p_cm
dev.off()
```

Feature graphs:

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

boruta_selected_features <- c(
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser",
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser",
  "diff2_acf1_AU01_inner_brow_raiser",
  "diff2_acf10_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "max_kl_shift_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "diff1_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "lumpiness_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "diff2_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"
)

# pretty_names <- c(
#   "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"       = "PACF(5) \u0394 AU17",
#   "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser" = "PACF(5) \u0394 AU01",
#   "diff2_acf1_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"   = "ACF(1) \u0394\u00B2 AU01",
#   "diff2_acf10_AU12_lip_corner_puller"  = "ACF(10) \u0394\u00B2 AU12",
#   "max_kl_shift_AU12_lip_corner_puller" = "Max KL Shift AU12",
#   "diff1_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "ACF(1) \u0394 AU12",
#   "lumpiness_AU12_lip_corner_puller"    = "Lumpiness AU12",
#   "diff2_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "ACF(1) \u0394\u00B2 AU12"
# )

pretty_names <- c(
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"       = "diff1x_pacf5_AU17",
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser" = "diff1x_pacf5_AU01",
  "diff2_acf1_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"   = "diff2_acf1_AU01",
  "diff2_acf10_AU12_lip_corner_puller"  = "diff2_acf10_AU12",
  "max_kl_shift_AU12_lip_corner_puller" = "max_kl_shift_AU12",
  "diff1_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "diff1_acf1_AU12",
  "lumpiness_AU12_lip_corner_puller"    = "lumpiness_AU12",
  "diff2_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "diff2_acf1_AU12"
)


plot_df <- dta_classifier_train_new_scaled %>%
  select(isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(boruta_selected_features),
    names_to = "Feature",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Feature = factor(Feature,
                     levels = boruta_selected_features,
                     labels = pretty_names)
  )

fill_pal <- c("real" = "#009E73", "fake" = "#E69F00")


# Save to PDF for paper
pdf("fig5_panel_C.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = isfakeorreal, y = Value, fill = isfakeorreal)) +
  
  # Violin plot
  geom_violin(
    width = 0.9, 
    trim = TRUE, 
    alpha = 0.3,
    color = "black", 
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  
  # Boxplot
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.25, 
    outlier.shape = 16, 
    outlier.size = 0.8,
    color = "black", 
    linewidth = 0.4, 
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Feature, 
    ncol = 2, 
    nrow = 4, 
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  
  # Apply colors and labels
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_pal, labels = c("Real", "Fake")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Final Selected Features",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Z-scored Value",
    fill = "Class"
  ) +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 10) + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = "grey50"),
    
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, color = "black"),
    
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey80", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )
dev.off()
```

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=5}
# --- 1. Final selected features (from your script) ---
boruta_selected_features <- c(
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser",
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser",
  "diff2_acf1_AU01_inner_brow_raiser",
  "diff2_acf10_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "max_kl_shift_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "diff1_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "lumpiness_AU12_lip_corner_puller",
  "diff2_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"
)

# --- 2. Pretty names for plot facets (using Unicode for Δ and ²) ---
pretty_names <- c(
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"       = "PACF(5) \u0394 AU17",
  "diff1x_pacf5_AU01_inner_brow_raiser" = "PACF(5) \u0394 AU01",
  "diff2_acf1_AU01_inner_brow_raiser"   = "ACF(1) \u0394\u00B2 AU01",
  "diff2_acf10_AU12_lip_corner_puller"  = "ACF(10) \u0394\u00B2 AU12",
  "max_kl_shift_AU12_lip_corner_puller" = "Max KL Shift AU12",
  "diff1_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "ACF(1) \u0394 AU12",
  "lumpiness_AU12_lip_corner_puller"    = "Lumpiness AU12",
  "diff2_acf1_AU12_lip_corner_puller"   = "ACF(1) \u0394\u00B2 AU12"
)

# --- 3. Prepare data for plotting ---
plot_df <- dta_classifier_train_new_scaled %>%
  select(isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(boruta_selected_features),
    names_to = "Feature",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  # Apply the pretty names and set factor order
  mutate(
    Feature = factor(Feature,
                     levels = boruta_selected_features,
                     labels = pretty_names),
    # Ensure consistent factor levels for isfakeorreal
    isfakeorreal = factor(isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
  )
plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Feature) %>%
  filter(
    Value >= quantile(Value, 0.005, na.rm = TRUE),
    Value <= quantile(Value, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# --- 4. Define color palette ---
fill_pal <- c("real" = "#1b9e77", "fake" = "#d95f02")

# --- 5. Create the 2x4 plot ---
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = isfakeorreal, y = Value, fill = isfakeorreal)) +
  
  # Violin plot
  geom_violin(
    width = 0.8, 
    trim = TRUE, 
    alpha = 0.3,
    color = "black", 
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  
  # Boxplot
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.25, 
    outlier.shape = 16, 
    outlier.size = 0.5,
    outlier.alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black", 
    linewidth = 0.35, 
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Facet into 2 columns and 4 rows
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Feature, 
    ncol = 2, 
    nrow = 4, 
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  
  # Apply colors and labels
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_pal, 
    labels = c("real" = "Real", "fake" = "Fake")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c("real" = "Real", "fake" = "Fake")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Final Selected Features",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Standardised Value (z-score)",
    fill = "Video Class"
  ) +
  
  # --- 6. Theme optimized for publication ---
  theme_classic(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    # Font family
    text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
    
    # Legend
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 3, 0),
    
    # Facet titles (the feature names)
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.3),
    
    # Title and axis labels
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    
    # Axis text
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7, color = "black"),
    
    # Panel
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    
    # Lines
    axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    
    # Margins
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
  )

print(p)


ggsave(
  "feature_distributions.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 5,
  height = 5,
  device = cairo_pdf
)

```

AU Before and After Smoothing By Real and Fake:

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=10}
dta_agg_long <- dta_preprocessed_smth %>%
  # Make sure to select the new variable
  select(frame, AU17_r, smth_AU17_r, isfakeorreal) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(AU17_r, smth_AU17_r),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Intensity"
  ) %>%
  group_by(isfakeorreal, frame, Type) %>%
  # mean intensity for real and fake
  summarise(
    Intensity = mean(Intensity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

base_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black")
  )

# Calculate common y axis range
y_limits <- range(dta_agg_long$Intensity, na.rm = T)

# plot 1
p_raw <- dta_agg_long %>%
  filter(Type == "AU17_r") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Intensity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#1b9e77", alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw",
    x = NULL,
    y = "AU Intensity"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  base_theme

# plot 2
p_smth <- dta_agg_long %>%
  filter(Type == "smth_AU17_r") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Intensity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#d95f02", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal) +
  labs(
    title = "Smoothed",
    x = "Frame",
    y = "AU Intensity"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  base_theme

# combine plots
p_raw / p_smth +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "AU17 - Before vs. After Smoothing", 
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, color = "black")
    )
  )
```

For two videos only:

```{r}
REAL_VIDEO_FILENAME <- "21__kitchen_still" 
FAKE_VIDEO_FILENAME <- "21_07__kitchen_still__MKU99DVX" 

dta_agg_long <- dta_preprocessed_smth %>%
  select(video_filename, frame, AU17_r, smth_AU17_r, isfakeorreal) %>% 
  filter(video_filename %in% c(REAL_VIDEO_FILENAME, FAKE_VIDEO_FILENAME)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(AU17_r, smth_AU17_r),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Intensity"
  )

base_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin  = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black")
  )

y_limits <- range(dta_agg_long$Intensity, na.rm = T)

# plot 1
p_raw <- dta_agg_long %>%
  filter(Type == "AU17_r") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Intensity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#1b9e77", alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw",
    x = NULL,
    y = "AU Intensity"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  base_theme

# plot 2
p_smth <- dta_agg_long %>%
  filter(Type == "smth_AU17_r") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Intensity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#d95f02", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal) +
  labs(
    title = "Smoothed",
    x = "Frame",
    y = "AU Intensity"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  base_theme

# combine plots
p_raw / p_smth +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "AU17 - Before vs. After Smoothing", 
    subtitle = "21_07__kitchen_still__MKU99DVX + 21__kitchen_still",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, color = "black")
    )
  )
```

Feature bar charts:

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}
REAL_VIDEO_FILENAME <- "21__kitchen_still" 
FAKE_VIDEO_FILENAME <- "21_07__kitchen_still__MKU99DVX" 

au17_feature <- "diff1x_pacf5_AU17_chin_raiser"

dta_feature_compare <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  filter(video_filename %in% c(REAL_VIDEO_FILENAME, FAKE_VIDEO_FILENAME)) %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, all_of(au17_feature)) %>%
  rename(Value = all_of(au17_feature))


ggplot(dta_feature_compare, aes(x = isfakeorreal, y = Value, fill = isfakeorreal)) +
  
  # Use geom_col() for a bar chart
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black") +
  
  # Add text labels to show the exact value
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Value, 2)), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  
  # Use the same colors as your other plots
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("real" = "#1b9e77", "fake" = "#d95f02"),
    guide = "none" # Hide the legend, as the x-axis is clear
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = au17_feature,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Feature Value (Z-scored)"
  ) +
  
  # Use a theme similar to your others
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16)
  )
```

# Classifiers w/ Balanced Emotion

## Random Forest

```{r}
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_balanced_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneLength = 5
)

print(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
rf_model_1_balanced_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_balanced_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

```         
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   33   16
      real   14   31
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6809          
                 95% CI : (0.5767, 0.7733)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.000294        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.3617          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.855132        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.7021          
            Specificity : 0.6596          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6735          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6889          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3511          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5213          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6809          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
Area under the curve: 0.6815
95% CI: 0.5707-0.7923 (DeLong)
```

### Sensitivity Analysis - Emotion Subsets with Balanced Model

```{r}
cat("--- EMOTION VIDEOS (n=58) ---\n")

# Get predictions on emotion subset
rf_balanced_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_balanced_emotion)

# Calculate ROC-AUC for emotion subset
y_test_emotion <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
p_fake_emotion <- predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, 
                          newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion, 
                          type = "prob")[, "fake"]

roc_obj_emotion <- pROC::roc(y_test_emotion, p_fake_emotion, levels = c("real", "fake"))
auc_emotion <- pROC::auc(roc_obj_emotion)
ci_emotion <- pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj_emotion)

cat(sprintf("\nROC-AUC: %.4f\n", auc_emotion))
cat(sprintf("95%% CI: %.4f - %.4f\n\n", ci_emotion[1], ci_emotion[3]))
```

No emotion:

```{r}
cat("--- NO EMOTION VIDEOS (n=36) ---\n")

# Get predictions on no emotion subset
rf_balanced_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_balanced_no_emotion)

# Calculate ROC-AUC for no emotion subset
y_test_no_emotion <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
p_fake_no_emotion <- predict(rf_fit_model1_balanced_cv, 
                             newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion, 
                             type = "prob")[, "fake"]

roc_obj_no_emotion <- pROC::roc(y_test_no_emotion, p_fake_no_emotion, levels = c("real", "fake"))
auc_no_emotion <- pROC::auc(roc_obj_no_emotion)
ci_no_emotion <- pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj_no_emotion)

cat(sprintf("\nROC-AUC: %.4f\n", auc_no_emotion))
cat(sprintf("95%% CI: %.4f - %.4f\n\n", ci_no_emotion[1], ci_no_emotion[3]))
```

Comparison:

```{r}
cat("ORIGINAL MODEL (trained on imbalanced data: 222 emotion, 148 no emotion):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Emotion videos (n=58):    Accuracy = %.1f%%, Sensitivity = %.1f%%, Specificity = %.1f%%\n",
            rf_model_1_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  No Emotion videos (n=36): Accuracy = %.1f%%, Sensitivity = %.1f%%, Specificity = %.1f%%\n",
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  >> Performance gap: %.1f%% accuracy difference\n\n",
            (rf_model_1_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] - rf_model_1_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"]) * 100))

cat("BALANCED MODEL (trained on balanced data: 222 emotion, 222 no emotion):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Emotion videos (n=58):    Accuracy = %.1f%%, Sensitivity = %.1f%%, Specificity = %.1f%%\n",
            rf_balanced_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_balanced_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_balanced_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  No Emotion videos (n=36): Accuracy = %.1f%%, Sensitivity = %.1f%%, Specificity = %.1f%%\n",
            rf_balanced_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_balanced_no_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_balanced_no_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  >> Performance gap: %.1f%% accuracy difference\n\n",
            (rf_balanced_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] - rf_balanced_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"]) * 100))


```

## C5.0B

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

c50_fit_model1_balanced_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_balanced_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = c(1, 10, 20, 30),  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

print(c50_fit_model1_balanced_cv)
plot(caret::varImp(c50_fit_model1_balanced_cv)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
c50_model_1_balanced_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_balanced_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(c50_fit_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

```         
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   30   23
      real   17   24
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5745          
                 95% CI : (0.4682, 0.6759)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.08983         
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1489          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.42920         
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6383          
            Specificity : 0.5106          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5660          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5854          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3191          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.5638          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5745          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
Area under the curve: 0.56
95% CI: 0.4423-0.6776 (DeLong)
```

## SVM

```{r}
# Cross-valiation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

svm_model1_balanced_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_balanced_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
svm_model_1_balanced_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_balanced_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(svm_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

```         
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   30   14
      real   17   33
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6702          
                 95% CI : (0.5656, 0.7638)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.0006307       
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.3404          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.7194375       
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6383          
            Specificity : 0.7021          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6818          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6600          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3191          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.4681          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6702          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
Area under the curve: 0.713
95% CI: 0.608-0.818 (DeLong)
```

## Logistic Regression

```{r}
# Cross-valiation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

lr_model1_balanced_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_balanced_scaled[, -(1:2)],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)

plot(caret::varImp(lr_model1_balanced_cv)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
lr_model1_balanced_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_balanced_cv_performance)


# ROC AUC
y_test  <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real","fake"))
p_fake  <- predict(lr_model1_balanced_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[,"fake"]

roc_obj <- pROC::roc(y_test, p_fake, levels = c("real","fake"))
pROC::auc(roc_obj)       # ROC-AUC
pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj)    # 95% CI (DeLong)
```

```         
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   22   21
      real   25   26
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5106          
                 95% CI : (0.4054, 0.6152)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.4590          
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.0213          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.6583          
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.4681          
            Specificity : 0.5532          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5116          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.5098          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.2340          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.4574          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5106          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
Area under the curve: 0.5405
95% CI: 0.4226-0.6584 (DeLong)
```

# Classifiers w/ Balanced Emotion Classes (Downsampling)

Previously I balanced the emotion class using bootstrap oversampling. I did this to prevent discarding many rows of data, however, this oversampling method works by adding duplicate rows to upsampling the minority class. A reviewer could argue that the classifier trained on this balanced train set is not actually learning anything different as the upsampled rows are the same. Therefore, I will run this again but this time using downsampling. This will discard data but will allow a fair comparison. Classes should be n=148 each after downsampling.

```{r}
set.seed(12345)
# Separate by emotion class
emotion_majority <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  filter(emotion == "yes")  # n = 222

emotion_minority <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  filter(emotion == "no")   # n = 148

cat(sprintf("Before downsampling:\n  Emotion: %d\n  No Emotion: %d\n\n",
            nrow(emotion_majority), nrow(emotion_minority)))

# Downsample majority (Emotion) to match minority (No Emotion)
emotion_majority_downsampled <- emotion_majority %>%
  sample_n(size = nrow(emotion_minority), replace = FALSE)

# Combine
dta_balanced_downsampled <- bind_rows(emotion_majority_downsampled, emotion_minority)

cat(sprintf("After downsampling:\n  Emotion: %d\n  No Emotion: %d\n  Total: %d\n\n",
            sum(dta_balanced_downsampled$emotion == "yes"),
            sum(dta_balanced_downsampled$emotion == "no"),
            nrow(dta_balanced_downsampled)))
```

```{r}
dta_train_ds <- dta_balanced_downsampled %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

# scale using same params as origina model
feature_columns <- names(dta_train_ds)[!names(dta_train_ds) %in% 
                                         c("video_filename", "emotion", "isfakeorreal")]

dta_train_ds_scaled <- dta_train_ds
dta_train_ds_scaled[, feature_columns] <- predict(scaling_model,
                                                    newdata = dta_train_ds[, feature_columns])

# Set factor levels
dta_train_ds_scaled$isfakeorreal <- factor(
  dta_train_ds_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  levels = c("fake", "real")
)

# train rf model

classifier_control_ds <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_downsampled_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_train_ds_scaled[, -(1:2)],  # exclude video_filename and emotion
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control_ds,
  tuneLength = 5
)

cat("--- DOWNSAMPLED RF MODEL ---\n")
print(rf_downsampled_cv)
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
library(pROC)
library(caret)

cat("\n--- FULL TEST SET (n=94) ---\n")
rf_ds_full <- confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
```
