```{r}
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)
print(rf_ds_full)

y_full <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
p_full <- predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled, type = "prob")[, "fake"]
roc_full <- pROC::roc(y_full, p_full, levels = c("real", "fake"))
cat(sprintf("ROC-AUC: %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n\n",
            pROC::auc(roc_full), ci.auc(roc_full)[1], ci.auc(roc_full)[3]))


cat("--- EMOTION VIDEOS (n=58) ---\n")
rf_ds_emotion <- confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)
print(rf_ds_emotion)

y_em <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
p_em <- predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion, type = "prob")[, "fake"]
roc_em <- pROC::roc(y_em, p_em, levels = c("real", "fake"))
auc_em <- pROC::auc(roc_em)
ci_em <- ci.auc(roc_em)
cat(sprintf("ROC-AUC: %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n\n", auc_em, ci_em[1], ci_em[3]))


cat("--- NO EMOTION VIDEOS (n=36) ---\n")
rf_ds_no_emotion <- confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)
print(rf_ds_no_emotion)

y_no <- factor(dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal, levels = c("real", "fake"))
p_no <- predict(rf_downsampled_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion, type = "prob")[, "fake"]
roc_no <- roc(y_no, p_no, levels = c("real", "fake"))
auc_no <- auc(roc_no)
ci_no <- ci.auc(roc_no)
cat(sprintf("ROC-AUC: %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n\n", auc_no, ci_no[1], ci_no[3]))
```

```         
--- FULL TEST SET (n=94) ---
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   35   22
      real   12   25
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6383          
                 95% CI : (0.5327, 0.7349)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.004774        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.2766          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.122713        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.7447          
            Specificity : 0.5319          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6140          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6757          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3723          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.6064          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6383          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
ROC-AUC: 0.6573 [0.5451, 0.7695]

--- EMOTION VIDEOS (n=58) ---
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   19    9
      real   10   20
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.6724          
                 95% CI : (0.5366, 0.7899)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.005964        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.3448          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1.000000        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.6552          
            Specificity : 0.6897          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.6786          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.6667          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.3276          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.4828          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.6724          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
ROC-AUC: 0.7265 [0.5964, 0.8566]

--- NO EMOTION VIDEOS (n=36) ---
Confusion Matrix and Statistics

          Reference
Prediction fake real
      fake   16   13
      real    2    5
                                          
               Accuracy : 0.5833          
                 95% CI : (0.4076, 0.7449)
    No Information Rate : 0.5             
    P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.202516        
                                          
                  Kappa : 0.1667          
                                          
 Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.009823        
                                          
            Sensitivity : 0.8889          
            Specificity : 0.2778          
         Pos Pred Value : 0.5517          
         Neg Pred Value : 0.7143          
             Prevalence : 0.5000          
         Detection Rate : 0.4444          
   Detection Prevalence : 0.8056          
      Balanced Accuracy : 0.5833          
                                          
       'Positive' Class : fake            
                                          
Setting direction: controls < cases
ROC-AUC: 0.5463 [0.3434, 0.7491]
```

Comparison:

```{r}

cat("================================================================\n")
cat("COMPARISON: ORIGINAL vs DOWNSAMPLED MODEL\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")

cat("ORIGINAL MODEL (trained on: 222 Emotion, 148 No Emotion = 370 total):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Emotion (n=58):    Acc = %.1f%%, Sens = %.1f%%, Spec = %.1f%%, AUC = 0.765\n",
            rf_model_1_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  No Emotion (n=36): Acc = %.1f%%, Sens = %.1f%%, Spec = %.1f%%, AUC = 0.605\n",
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_model_1_no_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  >> Gap: %.1f pp\n\n",
            (rf_model_1_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] - 
               rf_model_1_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"]) * 100))

cat(sprintf("DOWNSAMPLED MODEL (trained on: 148 Emotion, 148 No Emotion = 296 total):\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Emotion (n=58):    Acc = %.1f%%, Sens = %.1f%%, Spec = %.1f%%, AUC = %.3f\n",
            rf_ds_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_ds_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_ds_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100,
            auc_em))
cat(sprintf("  No Emotion (n=36): Acc = %.1f%%, Sens = %.1f%%, Spec = %.1f%%, AUC = %.3f\n",
            rf_ds_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] * 100,
            rf_ds_no_emotion$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100,
            rf_ds_no_emotion$byClass["Specificity"] * 100,
            auc_no))
cat(sprintf("  >> Gap: %.1f pp\n",
            (rf_ds_emotion$overall["Accuracy"] - 
               rf_ds_no_emotion$overall["Accuracy"]) * 100))
```

Pattern holds. The gap is less (16.9 to 8.9 pp) but key finding is preserved (emotion videos are significantly above chance (67.2%, p = .006) whereas no emotion videos remain non-significant (58.3%, p =.203)).

# Emotion Classification Model

To test whether the emotional signal itself is weaker in fakes.

```{r}
# Filter training data to include ONLY real videos
dta_emotion_train_real_only <- dta_classifier_train_new_scaled %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "real")

cat(sprintf("Original training set size: %d videos\n", nrow(dta_classifier_train_new_scaled)))
cat(sprintf("Real-only training set size: %d videos\n", nrow(dta_emotion_train_real_only)))

# Check class distribution
emotion_distribution <- table(dta_emotion_train_real_only$emotion)
cat("\nEmotion distribution in real-only training data:\n")
print(emotion_distribution)
```

Balance emotion class

```{r}
# Determine majority and minority classes
if (emotion_distribution["yes"] > emotion_distribution["no"]) {
  majority_class <- "yes"
  minority_class <- "no"
  n_majority <- emotion_distribution["yes"]
  n_minority <- emotion_distribution["no"]
} else {
  majority_class <- "no"
  minority_class <- "yes"
  n_majority <- emotion_distribution["no"]
  n_minority <- emotion_distribution["yes"]
}

cat(sprintf("\nMajority class: %s (n=%d)\n", majority_class, n_majority))
cat(sprintf("Minority class: %s (n=%d)\n", minority_class, n_minority))

# Option 1: Undersample majority class (recommended - no data duplication)
set.seed(12345)
majority_data <- dta_emotion_train_real_only %>%
  filter(emotion == majority_class)
minority_data <- dta_emotion_train_real_only %>%
  filter(emotion == minority_class)

# Randomly sample from majority to match minority size
majority_data_downsampled <- majority_data %>%
  sample_n(size = n_minority, replace = FALSE)

# Combine to create balanced dataset
dta_emotion_train_balanced <- bind_rows(majority_data_downsampled, minority_data) %>%
  arrange(video_filename)  # Shuffle is done in train() function

cat(sprintf("\nBalanced training set size: %d videos\n", nrow(dta_emotion_train_balanced)))
cat("\nBalanced emotion distribution:\n")
print(table(dta_emotion_train_balanced$emotion))

# Convert emotion to factor with explicit levels
dta_emotion_train_balanced$emotion <- factor(
  dta_emotion_train_balanced$emotion,
  levels = c("no", "yes")
)
```

Train classifier:

```{r}
emotion_classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

# Train Random Forest to predict EMOTION (not isfakeorreal)
set.seed(12345)

cat("\nTraining Random Forest to classify emotion (yes/no) using only real videos...\n")

rf_emotion_classifier <- caret::train(
  emotion ~ .,  # Predict emotion instead of isfakeorreal
  data = dta_emotion_train_balanced[, !names(dta_emotion_train_balanced) %in% 
                                      c("video_filename", "isfakeorreal")],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = emotion_classifier_control,
  tuneLength = 5
)

print(rf_emotion_classifier)

# Plot feature importance
cat("\nFeature Importance for Emotion Classification:\n")
plot(caret::varImp(rf_emotion_classifier))
```

Evaluate on REAL test data:

```{r}
dta_test_real_only <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "real")

cat(sprintf("Number of real videos in test set: %d\n", nrow(dta_test_real_only)))

# Ensure emotion is a factor with correct levels
dta_test_real_only$emotion <- factor(
  dta_test_real_only$emotion,
  levels = c("no", "yes")
)

# Get predictions
emotion_pred_real <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_emotion_classifier, newdata = dta_test_real_only),
  reference = dta_test_real_only$emotion,
  positive = "yes"
)

cat("\nPerformance on REAL videos:\n")
print(emotion_pred_real)

# Calculate ROC-AUC for real videos
y_test_real <- factor(dta_test_real_only$emotion, levels = c("no", "yes"))
p_emotion_real <- predict(rf_emotion_classifier, 
                          newdata = dta_test_real_only, 
                          type = "prob")[, "yes"]

roc_obj_real <- pROC::roc(y_test_real, p_emotion_real, levels = c("no", "yes"))
auc_real <- pROC::auc(roc_obj_real)
ci_real <- pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj_real)

cat(sprintf("\nROC-AUC on REAL videos: %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n", 
            auc_real, ci_real[1], ci_real[3]))
```

Evaluate on FAKE test data:

```{r}
cat("\n\n--- Step 5: Testing on FAKE Videos ---\n")

# Filter test set to fake videos only
dta_test_fake_only <- dta_classifier_test_new_scaled %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "fake")

cat(sprintf("Number of fake videos in test set: %d\n", nrow(dta_test_fake_only)))

# Ensure emotion is a factor with correct levels
dta_test_fake_only$emotion <- factor(
  dta_test_fake_only$emotion,
  levels = c("no", "yes")
)

# Get predictions
emotion_pred_fake <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_emotion_classifier, newdata = dta_test_fake_only),
  reference = dta_test_fake_only$emotion,
  positive = "yes"
)

cat("\nPerformance on FAKE videos:\n")
print(emotion_pred_fake)

# Calculate ROC-AUC for fake videos
y_test_fake <- factor(dta_test_fake_only$emotion, levels = c("no", "yes"))
p_emotion_fake <- predict(rf_emotion_classifier, 
                          newdata = dta_test_fake_only, 
                          type = "prob")[, "yes"]

roc_obj_fake <- pROC::roc(y_test_fake, p_emotion_fake, levels = c("no", "yes"))
auc_fake <- pROC::auc(roc_obj_fake)
ci_fake <- pROC::ci.auc(roc_obj_fake)

cat(sprintf("\nROC-AUC on FAKE videos: %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n", 
            auc_fake, ci_fake[1], ci_fake[3]))
```

Comparison:

```{r}
cat("\n\n=== COMPARISON: REAL vs FAKE VIDEOS ===\n\n")

cat("Emotion Classifier Performance:\n")
cat("(Trained on REAL videos only, tested on both REAL and FAKE)\n\n")

cat(sprintf("REAL videos (n=%d):\n", nrow(dta_test_real_only)))
cat(sprintf("  Accuracy:    %.1f%%\n", emotion_pred_real$overall["Accuracy"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  Sensitivity: %.1f%% (correctly identifies emotion='yes')\n", 
            emotion_pred_real$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  Specificity: %.1f%% (correctly identifies emotion='no')\n", 
            emotion_pred_real$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  ROC-AUC:     %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n", auc_real, ci_real[1], ci_real[3]))

cat(sprintf("\nFAKE videos (n=%d):\n", nrow(dta_test_fake_only)))
cat(sprintf("  Accuracy:    %.1f%%\n", emotion_pred_fake$overall["Accuracy"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  Sensitivity: %.1f%% (correctly identifies emotion='yes')\n", 
            emotion_pred_fake$byClass["Sensitivity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  Specificity: %.1f%% (correctly identifies emotion='no')\n", 
            emotion_pred_fake$byClass["Specificity"] * 100))
cat(sprintf("  ROC-AUC:     %.4f [%.4f, %.4f]\n", auc_fake, ci_fake[1], ci_fake[3]))

# Calculate performance drop
accuracy_drop <- (emotion_pred_real$overall["Accuracy"] - 
                  emotion_pred_fake$overall["Accuracy"]) * 100
auc_drop <- auc_real - auc_fake

cat(sprintf("\n>> PERFORMANCE DROP on FAKE videos:\n"))
cat(sprintf("   Accuracy: %.1f%% lower\n", accuracy_drop))
cat(sprintf("   ROC-AUC:  %.4f lower\n", auc_drop))
```

Statistical tests:

```{r}
# Fisher's exact test to compare accuracy between real and fake
real_correct <- sum(emotion_pred_real$table[1,1], emotion_pred_real$table[2,2])
real_incorrect <- sum(emotion_pred_real$table[1,2], emotion_pred_real$table[2,1])
fake_correct <- sum(emotion_pred_fake$table[1,1], emotion_pred_fake$table[2,2])
fake_incorrect <- sum(emotion_pred_fake$table[1,2], emotion_pred_fake$table[2,1])

contingency_emotion <- matrix(c(real_correct, real_incorrect,
                                fake_correct, fake_incorrect),
                              nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE,
                              dimnames = list(c("Real", "Fake"),
                                            c("Correct", "Incorrect")))

cat("Contingency table for accuracy:\n")
print(contingency_emotion)

fisher_emotion <- fisher.test(contingency_emotion)
cat(sprintf("\nFisher's exact test (Real vs Fake accuracy): p = %.4f\n", 
            fisher_emotion$p.value))

# McNemar's test is not appropriate here since we're comparing different videos
# But we can do a proportion test
prop_test_emotion <- prop.test(x = c(real_correct, fake_correct),
                               n = c(nrow(dta_test_real_only), nrow(dta_test_fake_only)))
cat(sprintf("Two-proportion z-test (Real vs Fake accuracy): p = %.4f\n", 
            prop_test_emotion$p.value))
```
